Event: Avon outmaneuvers Daniels during a car tailing
Explanation: Evades police surveillance, protecting himself while hampering law enforcement.

Event: Avon figures out Marlo’s long-term plan from a brief inmate conversation
Explanation: Gains critical strategic insight that helps his position and threatens rivals’ stability.

Event: Avon finger-wags Daniels after shaking off the police tail
Explanation: Minor ego boost and assertion of power without real added harm beyond what evasion already caused.

Event: Avon orchestrates a mass poisoning in prison to force early release
Explanation: Greatly advances his freedom at the cost of lethal harm to other inmates.

Event: Avon offers to testify against the “culprit” of the poisoning to secure early release
Explanation: Uses testimony to get out early, sacrificing the guard for his own gain.

Event: Avon runs a generally benevolent, rewarding regime for his crew
Explanation: His fair treatment strengthens loyalty and operational success for everyone in the organization.

Event: Avon wages war against Marlo to defend his pride and territory
Explanation: His stubborn war depletes his power base and draws heavy police heat with little gain.

Event: Avon orders murders as routine tool of business
Explanation: Consolidates his power and profits by inflicting lethal harm on others.

Event: Avon has a girlfriend killed for threatening to go to the cops
Explanation: Removes a legal threat and protects himself at the cost of her life and later prosecution risk.

Event: Avon surrenders peacefully during his first major arrest and has Stringer open the safe
Explanation: Minimizes property loss and danger while letting police do their job more safely.

Event: Avon calmly waits in the nightclub for police to arrive at his arrest
Explanation: Accepts inevitable arrest without violence, reducing risk to himself and officers.

Event: Avon orders his soldiers not to fight back when police surround the armory
Explanation: Prevents a bloody shootout, limiting casualties and catastrophic charges for his men.

Event: Avon deliberately crosses the prison baseball field mid-play to make everyone stop
Explanation: Affirms his dominance and ego while disrupting others’ recreation and reinforcing fear.

Event: Avon pays for permanent care of his comatose brother
Explanation: Spends significant resources with no material gain to himself while greatly benefiting his brother’s care.

Event: Avon arranges the death of Stringer Bell despite knowing it will hurt profits
Explanation: Sacrifices a highly competent partner and business strength for revenge with net strategic loss.

Event: Avon condemns Stringer’s plan to hit Omar while escorting his grandmother
Explanation: Protects his own “standards” reputation and reduces backlash from an especially offensive hit.

Event: Avon mostly refrains from killing over insults except in Omar’s case
Explanation: Shows restraint on low-value killings, slightly benefiting his stability and others’ survival.

Event: Avon recognizes the Co-Op’s negotiation plan for Marlo is futile and rejects it
Explanation: Improves his own strategic read and stance while undermining a flawed but less violent plan.

Event: Avon mixes family with business by sending D’Angelo on a risky run with no backup
Explanation: Endangers his heir and angers family, harming his organization and relations with little upside.

Event: Avon sets Tilghman up to smuggle tainted drugs into prison
Explanation: Builds leverage for parole by causing inmate deaths and framing a guard.

Event: Avon comes forward to name Tilghman as culprit to move up parole hearing
Explanation: Directly trades another man’s career and freedom for advancement of his own release.

Event: Avon maintains control of his business and prison drug supply while incarcerated
Explanation: Keeps profits and comfort flowing at the expense of wider social harm from the drug trade.

Event: Avon spends prison time playing video games and eating KFC via influence
Explanation: Secures personal comfort and status by corrupting/profiting from lax oversight.

Event: Avon allows Cutty to leave the game and gives him money to start a boxing gym
Explanation: Spends modest capital and loses one soldier but gains moral capital and community improvement.

Event: Avon reacts to Omar’s attempted hit by getting drunk with Stringer and Wee-Bey
Explanation: Copes in a self-destructive way that offers short-term relief but no strategic benefit.

Event: Avon doubles the bounty on Omar upon learning he’s gay
Explanation: Increases incentive to kill a major enemy from prejudice, aiding his safety while inflaming targeted harm.

Event: Avon implements advanced communication protocols to avoid police detection
Explanation: Makes his empire more secure and profitable while significantly impeding law enforcement.

Event: Avon tampers with project records to replace his image with a white man’s
Explanation: Further shields himself from identification, hampering investigations.

Event: Avon mentors D’Angelo to be his worthy successor
Explanation: Invests in grooming competent leadership, helping both his heir and long-term stability.

Event: Avon refuses Stringer’s “legit” strategy and insists on violent corner dominance
Explanation: Clings to a fading model that attracts heat and undermines longer-term, safer profit strategies.

Event: Avon sponsors a local charity basketball game
Explanation: Buys goodwill and reputation while giving a genuine community benefit.

Event: Avon gives Cutty further financial backing to get his boxing gym running
Explanation: Invests in positive community infrastructure with reputational and relational gains.

Event: Avon vows never to go back to prison after his first release
Explanation: His determination is undercut by reckless choices that send him back, negating any benefit.

Event: Avon experiences a Villainous BSoD after Stringer’s death, losing interest in his war
Explanation: His depression cripples his leadership, hurting his power while easing the gang war somewhat.

Event: Avon accepts arrest and laughs at the end of his criminal career in Season 3 finale
Explanation: Resigns himself with no real resistance, limiting danger to others but sealing his own downfall.

Event: Avon cultivates close, loyal relationships with Stringer and Wee-Bey
Explanation: Mutual loyalty strengthens operations and provides emotional support to all three.

Event: Avon comes to view McNulty as a worthy opponent and adjusts to his presence
Explanation: Respecting the threat sharpens his defensive strategies and raises the game for both sides.
